1.0.0.3 false ACCOUNTING POLICIES false 1 $ false false Shares Standard http://www.xbrl.org/2003/instance shares xbrli 0 USD Standard http://www.xbrl.org/2003/iso4217 USD iso4217 0 USDPerShare Divide http://www.xbrl.org/2003/iso4217 USD iso4217 http://www.xbrl.org/2003/instance shares xbrli 0 2 NOTES TO CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS 0 kr_NotesToConsolidatedFinancialsAbstract kr false na duration string No definition available. false false false false false true false false false 1 false false 0 0 false false No definition available. false 3 ACCOUNTING POLICIES 1 us-gaap_SignificantAccountingPoliciesTextBlock us-gaap true na duration string No definition available. false false false false false false false false false 1 false false 0 0 <table style="font-size:10pt; font-family:'Times New Roman',times,serif;"> <tr> <td> <p style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt 0.25in; TEXT-INDENT: -0.25in"> <b><font style="FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman" size="2">1.</font></b><b><font style="FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 3pt" size="1">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;</font></b> <b><font style="FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt" size="2">A</font></b><b><font style="FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt" size="2">CCOUNTING</font></b> <b><font style="FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt" size="2">P</font></b><b><font style="FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt" size="2">OLICIES</font></b></p> <p style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt 0.25in; TEXT-INDENT: -0.25in"> &nbsp;</p> <p style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; COLOR: black; TEXT-INDENT: 9pt; FONT-STYLE: italic"> <i><font style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; COLOR: windowtext; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman" color="black" size="2">Basis of Presentation and Principles of Consolidation</font></i></p> <p style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; COLOR: black; TEXT-INDENT: 9pt; FONT-STYLE: italic"> &nbsp;</p> <p style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"> <font style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman" size="2">The accompanying financial statements include the consolidated accounts of The Kroger Co., its wholly-owned subsidiaries, and the Variable Interest Entities (&#147;VIE&#148;) in which the Company is the primary beneficiary.&nbsp; The January&nbsp;31, 2009 balance sheet was derived from audited financial statements, adjusted for the adoption of the new standards for the Company&#146;s noncontrolling interest in a subsidiary and, due to its summary nature, does not include all disclosures required by generally accepted accounting principles (&#147;GAAP&#148;). Significant intercompany transactions and balances have been eliminated.&nbsp; References to the &#147;Company&#148; in these Consolidated Financial Statements mean the consolidated company.</font></p> <p style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in">&nbsp;</p> <p style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"> <font style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman" size="2">In the opinion of management, the accompanying unaudited Consolidated Financial Statements include all normal, recurring adjustments that are necessary for a fair presentation of results of operations for such periods but should not be considered as indicative of results for a full year. The financial statements have been prepared by the Company pursuant to the rules&nbsp;and regulations of the Securities and Exchange Commission (&#147;SEC&#148;). Certain information and footnote disclosures normally included in financial statements prepared in accordance with GAAP have been omitted, pursuant to SEC regulations. Accordingly, the accompanying Consolidated Financial Statements should be read in conjunction with the Annual Report on Form&nbsp;10-K of The Kroger Co. for the fiscal year ended January&nbsp;31, 2009.</font></p> <p style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in">&nbsp;</p> <p style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"> <font style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman" size="2">The unaudited information in the Consolidated Financial Statements for the third quarter and three quarters ended November&nbsp;7, 2009 and November&nbsp;8, 2008 includes the results of operations of the Company for the 40-week periods then ended.</font></p> <p style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in">&nbsp;</p> <p style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"> <font style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman" size="2">In the first quarter of 2009, the Company adopted the new standards for a parent&#146;s noncontrolling interests in a subsidiary and applied it</font> <font style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt" size="2">retrospectively.&nbsp; As a result, the Company reclassified noncontrolling interests in amounts of $95 from the mezzanine section to equity in the January&nbsp;31, 2009 Consolidated Balance Sheet.&nbsp; Certain reclassifications to the Consolidated Statements of Operations have been made to prior period amounts to conform to the presentation of the current period under the new standards.&nbsp; Recorded amounts for prior periods previously presented as Net Earnings, which are now presented as Net Earnings Attributable to The Kroger Co., have not changed as a result of the adoption of the new standards.</font></p> <p style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in">&nbsp;</p> <p style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 9pt"> <i><font style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman" size="2">Impairment of Long-Lived Assets</font></i></p> <p style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 9pt">&nbsp;</p> <p style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; COLOR: black; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"> <font style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman" color="black" size="2">In accordance with GAAP, the Company monitors the carrying value of long-lived assets for potential impairment each quarter based on whether certain trigger events have occurred.&nbsp; These events include current period losses combined with a history of losses or a projection of continuing losses or a significant decrease in the market value of an asset.&nbsp; When a trigger event occurs, an impairment calculation is performed, comparing projected undiscounted future cash flows, utilizing current cash flow information and expected growth rates related to specific stores, to the carrying value for those stores.&nbsp; If the Company identifies impairment for long-lived assets to be held and used, the Company compares the assets&#146; current carrying value to the assets&#146; fair value.&nbsp; Fair value is based on current market values or discounted future cash flows.&nbsp; The Company records impairment when the carrying value exceeds fair market value.&nbsp; With respect to owned property and equipment held for sale, the value of the property and equipment is adjusted to reflect recoverable values based on previous efforts to dispose of similar assets and current economic conditions.&nbsp; Impairment is recognized for the excess of the carrying value over the estimated fair market value, reduced by estimated direct costs of disposal.&nbsp; The Company recorded asset impairments in the normal course of business totaling $29 in the third quarter of 2009 and $5 in the third quarter of 2008.&nbsp; During the first three quarters of 2009 and 2008, the Company recorded asset impairments in the normal course of business totaling $44 and $21, respectively.&nbsp; Included in these amounts are asset impairments recorded totaling $24 in both the third quarter and first three quarters of 2009 for a southern California reporting unit.&nbsp; Costs to reduce the carrying value of long-lived assets for each of the years presented have been included in the Consolidated Statements of Operations as operating, general and administrative expense.</font></p> <p style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-ALIGN: center" align="center"> &nbsp;</p> <p style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt 9pt"> <i><font style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-STYLE: italic; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman" size="2">Store Closing and Other Expense Allowances</font></i></p> <p style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt">&nbsp;</p> <p style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"> <font style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman" size="2">The Company provides for closed store liabilities relating to the present value of the estimated remaining noncancelable lease payments after the closing date, net of estimated subtenant income.&nbsp; The Company estimates the net lease liabilities using a discount rate to calculate the present value of the remaining net rent payments on closed stores.&nbsp; The closed store lease liabilities usually are paid over the lease terms associated with the closed stores, which generally have remaining terms ranging from one to 20 years.&nbsp; Adjustments to closed store liabilities primarily relate to changes in subtenant income and actual exit costs differing from original estimates.&nbsp; Adjustments are made for changes in estimates in the period in which the change becomes known.&nbsp; Store closing liabilities are reviewed quarterly to ensure that any accrued amount that is not a sufficient estimate of future costs, or that no longer is needed for its originally intended purpose, is adjusted to income in the proper period.</font></p> <p style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in">&nbsp;</p> <p style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"> <font style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman" size="2">Owned stores held for disposal are reduced to their estimated net realizable value.&nbsp; Costs to reduce the carrying values of property, equipment and leasehold improvements are accounted for in accordance with the Company&#146;s policy on impairment of long-lived assets.&nbsp; Inventory write-downs, if any, in connection with store closings, are classified in &#147;Merchandise costs.&#148;&nbsp; Costs to transfer inventory and equipment from closed stores are expensed as incurred.</font></p> <p style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in">&nbsp;</p> <p style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"> <font style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman" size="2">The following table summarizes accrual activity for future lease obligations of stores that were closed in the normal course of business and locations closed in California prior to the Fred Meyer merger in 1999.</font></p> <p style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; 1.0.0.3 false GUARANTOR SUBSIDIARIES false 1 $ false false Shares Standard http://www.xbrl.org/2003/instance shares xbrli 0 USD Standard http://www.xbrl.org/2003/iso4217 USD iso4217 0 USDPerShare Divide http://www.xbrl.org/2003/iso4217 USD iso4217 http://www.xbrl.org/2003/instance shares xbrli 0 2 NOTES TO CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS 0 kr_NotesToConsolidatedFinancialsAbstract kr false na duration string No definition available. false false false false false true false false false 1 false false 0 0 false false No definition available. false 3 GUARANTOR SUBSIDIARIES 1 us-gaap_ScheduleOfCondensedFinancialStatementsTextBlock us-gaap true na duration string No definition available. false false false false false false false false false 1 false false 0 0 <table style="font-size:10pt; font-family:'Times New Roman',times,serif;"> <tr> <td> <p style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt"> <b><font style="FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman" size="2">11.&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; G</font></b><b><font style="FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt" size="2">UARANTOR</font></b><b><font style="FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt" size="2">S</font></b><b><font style="FONT-WEIGHT: bold; FONT-SIZE: 10pt" size="2">UBSIDIARIES</font></b></p> <p style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt">&nbsp;</p> <p style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"> <font style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman" size="2">The Company&#146;s outstanding public debt (the &#147;Guaranteed Notes&#148;) is jointly and severally, fully and unconditionally guaranteed by The Kroger Co. and certain of its subsidiaries (the &#147;Guarantor Subsidiaries&#148;). At November&nbsp;7, 2009, a total of approximately $7.31 billion of Guaranteed Notes were outstanding. The Guarantor Subsidiaries and non-guarantor subsidiaries are direct or indirect subsidiaries of The Kroger Co. Separate financial statements of The Kroger Co. and each of the Guarantor Subsidiaries are not presented because the guarantees are full and unconditional and the Guarantor Subsidiaries are jointly and severally liable. The Company believes that separate financial statements and other disclosures concerning the Guarantor Subsidiaries would not be material to investors.</font></p> <p style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in">&nbsp;</p> <p style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"> <font style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman" size="2">The non-guaranteeing subsidiaries, including non-wholly owned entities, represent less than 3% on an individual and aggregate basis of consolidated assets, pre-tax earnings, cash flow, and equity. Therefore, the non-guarantor subsidiaries&#146; information, including non-wholly owned entities, is not separately presented and recorded amounts are included within the Guarantor Subsidiaries totals in the tables below.</font></p> <p style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt">&nbsp;</p> <p style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"> <font style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman" size="2">There are no current restrictions on the ability of the Guarantor Subsidiaries to make payments under the guarantees referred to above. The obligations of each guarantor under its guarantee are limited to the maximum amount permitted under Bankruptcy Law, the Uniform Fraudulent Conveyance Act, the Uniform Fraudulent Transfer Act, or any similar Federal or state law (e.g. laws requiring adequate capital to pay dividends) respecting fraudulent conveyance or fraudulent transfer.</font></p> <p style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-ALIGN: center" align="center"> &nbsp;</p> <p style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; TEXT-INDENT: 0.25in"> <font style="FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: Times New Roman" size="2">The following tables present summarized financial information as of November&nbsp;7, 2009 and January&nbsp;31, 2009 and for the third quarter, and three quarters ended November&nbsp;7, 2009 and November&nbsp;8, 2008:</font></p> <p style="MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt;
